title: Sindhu: 'I Kept Believing I Could Do It' After Japan Open Win
indian_angle: India's star shuttler PV Sindhu reflects on her journey after winning the Japan Open, ending a significant title drought.
summary: PV Sindhu expressed her unwavering belief in herself after defeating Akane Yamaguchi 21-17, 21-17 to claim her maiden Japan Open title, her first since 2024.

title: Delhi World Championships Venue Undergoing Major Revamp
indian_angle: Preparations for the World Championships in Delhi are underway with significant venue upgrades to ensure a world-class event in India.
summary: The venue for the upcoming World Badminton Championships in Delhi is undergoing a major revamp, with the BAI assuring that concerns regarding stray animals and facilities have been addressed.

title: Gopichand Praises Sindhu's Resurgence, Sees Her Beating Top Players
indian_angle: National coach Pullela Gopichand's positive assessment of PV Sindhu's form boosts confidence for her future performances for India.
summary: Pullela Gopichand expressed his belief in PV Sindhu's renewed ability to defeat top-ranked players, highlighting her strong comeback.

title: Sindhu Overcomes Chen Yufei in Japan Open Semifinal
indian_angle: PV Sindhu's victory over a top-ranked opponent in the Japan Open semifinal was a crucial step towards her title win, showcasing her improved form.
summary: PV Sindhu defeated World No. 4 Chen Yufei in the Japan Open semifinal, a significant win that ended her losing streak against the Chinese player.

title: New 3x15 Scoring Format to Debut in Indian Domestic Badminton
indian_angle: A significant rule change for all domestic badminton tournaments in India will debut in Ernakulam, potentially impacting player strategies.
summary: The Badminton Association of India is introducing a new 3x15 scoring format for all domestic tournaments, with its debut scheduled in Ernakulam.
